title: Mean, Median and Mode of Ungrouped Data
subject: Mathematics
learningOutcomes: Understand measures of central tendency: Mean, Median, and Mode.
Interactively adjust dataset values by dragging graph bars.

objectives.text: Calculate the mean of 5 subject scores.
Drag a bar to alter dataset values and observe shifts in median.

theory: Mean is the sum of all values divided by count ($n$). Median is the middle value when sorted. Mode is the value that occurs most frequently.
hints: Drag the bar tops up or down to edit values directly.
Use the Dataset Editor panel to add new categories or shuffle values.

tasks.instruction: Drag any graph bar to increase its value above 60.

observationPrompts: How does increasing an extreme value affect the Mean vs the Median?

realWorldExamples.title: Class Test Averages
realWorldExamples.description: Teachers calculate the mean score of a class to evaluate test performance.

commonMistakes: Forgetting to sort the data array before computing the Median.
